title: Mojtaba Khamenei, a new supreme leader consolidating the power of Iran's conservative factions
subhead: The appointment of Ali Khamenei's son, a little-known figure with close ties to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, signals the Iranian regime's intention to maintain the political status quo, as war with Israel and the US continues.

title: Macron announces France and allies preparing joint mission to reopen Strait of Hormuz
subhead: Speaking from Cyprus, an EU country that was struck by Iranian-made drones, Emmanuel Macron said the 'purely defensive, purely support mission' would be aimed at escorting container ships and tankers in order to gradually reopen the strait.

title: The United Arab Emirates is caught in the fog of war
subhead: Surprised by the scale of Iran's attacks on their territory, Emirati leaders have worked to downplay the impact in order to retain the confidence of investors and tourists, while continuing to place orders for weapons.

title: 'For women, remote work can reduce the career costs of motherhood'
subhead: If policymakers truly want to address declining birth rates, they should consider remote work as a valuable tool, argues economist Cevat Giray Aksoy.

title: 'War is far too sensitive an issue to be managed by artificial intelligence'
subhead: The standoff that erupted at the end of February in the US between Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth and Dario Amodei, the head of Anthropic, highlights the urgent need to establish a political and legal framework capable of governing the use of technology, writes Le Monde columnist Stéphane Lauer.

Iranian school hit by air strike: Le Monde's investigation confirms many civilian victims, including children
subhead: Videos obtained and analyzed by Le Monde confirmed that the bombing of a school in Minab, Iran, on the first day of joint US-Israeli strikes on the country, caused many civilian victims, including children.
byline: Alexandre Horn,  Asia Balluffier and  Ghazal Golshiri

title: Thomas Piketty: 'In the face of Trump's militaristic drift and looming disaster, Europe must equip itself to have global influence'
subhead: Following the intervention in Venezuela, the war against Iran launched by the US president signals yet another admission of weakness by the United States, observes the economist in his column. This military agitation primarily serves to bolster the US economy.

title: What the Greek myth of Pandora reveals about the deep-seated fear of women
subhead: By opening a jar entrusted to her by the gods, Pandora becomes the instrument of their vengeance against rebellious humans and unleashes a torrent of plagues upon her fellow mortals. This misogynistic myth seems to contain all the resentments one half of humanity harbors against the other.

title: Israel and war as the only prospect
